props.name: appearance
props.description: Defines the visual style of the Button.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose Radix's functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the component's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. asChild can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: icon
props.description: An icon to render inside the button. If the `state` is `"pending"`, then the icon will automatically be replaced with a spinner.

props.name: iconPlacement
props.description: The side that the icon will render on, if one is present. If `state="pending"`, then the loading icon will also render on this side.

props.name: isLoading
props.description: Whether or not the button is in a loading state, default `false`. Setting `isLoading` will replace any `icon` with a spinner, or add one if an icon wasn't given. It will also disable user interaction with the button and set `disabled`.

props.name: priority
props.description: Indicates the importance or impact level of the button, affecting its color and styling to communicate its purpose to the user

props.name: type
props.description: The default behavior of the button. Possible values are: `"button"`, `"submit"`, and `"reset"`. if `asChild` is NOT used: Unlike the native `<button>` element, this prop is required and has no default value. If `asChild` IS used: This prop HAS NO EFFECT, is REMOVED, and has no default value. This is because we do not want the `button` `type` to automatically merge with any child anchor `type` attribute because the `anchor` `type` is _strictly different_ than the `button` type, see: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#type

enumDoc.meaning: The button has no default behavior, and does nothing when pressed by default. It can have client-side scripts listen to the element's events, which are triggered when the events occur.
The button resets all the controls to their initial values.
The button submits the form data to the server.

props.branchInfo: Required when asChild is not set

When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.branchInfo: Required when asChild is not set

props.name: preferredWidth
props.description: The preferred width of the `AlertDialogContent` as a tailwind `max-w-` class. By default, a `AlertDialog`'s content width is responsive with a default preferred width: the maximum width of the `AlertDialogContent`

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose component functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose component functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: data-slot
props.description: Stable styling hook used by mantle to identify this element in the DOM (e.g. `data-slot="icon"`). Consumers can override it via props spread.

props.name: focusable
props.description: Whether the SVG can receive keyboard focus. Use the string form (`"true"` / `"false"`) — the SVG attribute is serialized as a string and React's boolean form can produce inconsistent output.

props.name: priority
props.description: Indicates the importance or impact level of the AlertDialog, affecting its color and styling to communicate its purpose to the user.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose component functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: icon
props.description: An icon to render inside the anchor

props.name: iconPlacement
props.description: The side that the icon will render on, if one is present

props.name: rel
props.description: The rel attribute defines the relationship between a linked resource and the current document. Every keyword within a space-separated value should be unique within that value.

props.name: appearance
props.description: The visual style of the badge.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose component functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: color
props.description: The color variant of the badge. Accepts named colors and functional colors from the mantle color palette.

props.name: icon
props.description: The icon to render inside the badge.

props.name: children
props.description: Children must be a render function so that evaluation is deferred until after hydration has occurred.

props.name: fallback
props.description: Optional fallback to render on the server (and during hydration) before the client-only children are mounted. Ideally, this should be the same dimensions as the eventual children to avoid layout shift.

props.name: checked
props.description: The controlled checked state of the checkbox. Must be used in conjunction with onChange.

props.name: defaultChecked
props.description: The checked state of the checkbox when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its checked state.

props.name: validation
props.description: Visual validation state for the control. `validation="error"` also maps to `aria-invalid=true` unless an explicit `aria-invalid` prop is present.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose component functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: code
props.description: The country code for the flag to display

props.name: loading
props.description: A string providing a hint to the user agent as to how to best schedule the loading of the image to optimize page performance.

props.name: size
props.description: The size of flag to render, "s", "m", or "l"

props.name: data-slot
props.description: Stable styling hook used by mantle to identify this element in the DOM (e.g. `data-slot="icon"`). Consumers can override it via props spread.

props.name: focusable
props.description: Whether the SVG can receive keyboard focus. Use the string form (`"true"` / `"false"`) — the SVG attribute is serialized as a string and React's boolean form can produce inconsistent output.

props.name: svg
props.description: A single SVG icon element.

props.name: autoComplete
props.description: (Not a Boolean attribute!) The autocomplete attribute takes as its value a space-separated string that describes what, if any, type of autocomplete functionality the input should provide. A typical implementation of autocomplete recalls previous values entered in the same input field, but more complex forms of autocomplete can exist. For instance, a browser could integrate with a device's contacts list to autocomplete email addresses in an email input field. The autocomplete attribute is valid on hidden, text, search, url, tel, email, date, month, week, time, datetime-local, number, range, color, and password. This attribute has no effect on input types that do not return numeric or text data, being valid for all input types except checkbox, radio, file, or any of the button types.

props.name: type
props.description: A string specifying the type of control to render. For example, to create a checkbox, a value of `"checkbox"` is used. If omitted (or an unknown value is specified), the input type `"text"` is used, creating a plaintext input field.

props.name: validation
props.description: Visual validation state for the control. `validation="error"` also maps to `aria-invalid=true` unless an explicit `aria-invalid` prop is present.

props.name: disabled
props.description: If set, the label will appear disabled.

props.name: allowClickEventDefault
props.description: Only call `event.preventDefault()` in the `onClick` handler if the user has not set `allowClickEventDefault` to `true`. This allows the user to control whether or not the default behavior of the click event should be allowed. This is useful for links or buttons that should navigate or perform some action on click.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose component functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose component functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: orientation
props.description: Either `horizontal` or `vertical`.

props.name: semantic
props.description: If `true`, the separator will be rendered with all accessibility-related attributes and role="separator". If `false`, the separator is purely decorative and all accessibility-related attributes are updated so that that the rendered element is removed from the accessibility tree.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the `asChild` prop to compose component functionality onto alternative element types or your own React components. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

When `asChild` is set to `true`, mantle will not render a default DOM element, instead cloning the part's child and passing it the props and behavior required to make it functional. `asChild` can be used as deeply as you need to. This means it is a great way to compose multiple primitive's behavior together.

props.name: targetId
props.description: The id of the target element to focus when the link is activated. Must match the `id` on the `<Main>` (or any focusable landmark) you want keyboard users to be sent to.

props.name: color
props.description: The color of the slider range. Accepts any Tailwind `bg-*` class.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The value of the slider when initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control the state of the slider. The values of the slider when initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control the state of the slider. Pass an array to create a range slider with multiple thumbs.

props.name: showTicks
props.description: Whether to show tick marks along the track at each `step` interval.

props.name: value
props.description: The controlled value of the slider. Must be used with `onValueChange`. The controlled values of the slider. Must be used with `onValueChange`. Pass an array to create a range slider with multiple thumbs.

props.name: color
props.description: The color of the slider range. Accepts any Tailwind `bg-*` class.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The value of the slider when initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control the state of the slider.

props.name: showTicks
props.description: Whether to show tick marks along the track at each `step` interval.

props.name: value
props.description: The controlled value of the slider. Must be used with `onValueChange`.

props.name: color
props.description: The color of the slider range. Accepts any Tailwind `bg-*` class.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The values of the slider when initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control the state of the slider. Pass an array to create a range slider with multiple thumbs.

props.name: showTicks
props.description: Whether to show tick marks along the track at each `step` interval.

props.name: value
props.description: The controlled values of the slider. Must be used with `onValueChange`. Pass an array to create a range slider with multiple thumbs.

props.name: readOnly
props.description: Makes the switch immutable, meaning the user can not edit the control.

props.name: validation
props.description: Visual validation state for the control. `validation="error"` also maps to `aria-invalid=true` unless an explicit `aria-invalid` prop is present.

props.name: appearance
props.description: The visual style of the textarea.

props.name: validation
props.description: Visual validation state for the control. `validation="error"` also maps to `aria-invalid=true` unless an explicit `aria-invalid` prop is present.
